purpose: Welcome screen rendered when ideWorkspaces is empty. Two action buttons: 'Open Folder' (uses window.electronAPI.ideSelectFolder native picker) and 'Create New Folder' (picker for parent + window.prompt for name + ideCreateFolder IPC). After picking/creating, generates a workspace ID (String(Date.now())), dispatches IDE_OPEN_WORKSPACE { id, path, name }, then calls createWorkspaceDashboard(wsId) and saveDashboardProject(dashboardId, folderPath) to ensure a dashboard exists for chat/swarm context.
gotchas: Workspace ID is String(Date.now()) — two simultaneous 'Open Folder' clicks within 1ms could collide (rare but theoretically possible).
Create New Folder uses window.prompt() — synchronous, blocks UI, no validation beyond non-empty trim.

Path concatenation strips trailing slashes via .replace(/\/+$/, '') then appends '/' + name — assumes POSIX paths; on Windows the picker should still return forward slashes through Electron, but subdirectories would still mix separators if user enters '\\'.

Does NOT dispatch IDE_LINK_WORKSPACE_DASHBOARD — relies on getWorkspaceDashboard(wsId) lookup later. Compare to WorkspaceTabs.handleAddWorkspace which DOES dispatch IDE_LINK_WORKSPACE_DASHBOARD — divergent behavior between the two folder-open paths.
Errors in createWorkspaceDashboard are logged but not surfaced to user — workspace opens with no dashboard silently.
patterns: Dual entry point for opening workspaces: this component (cold-start welcome) and WorkspaceTabs (when workspaces exist). Both should use the same IDE_OPEN_WORKSPACE + createWorkspaceDashboard sequence.
Folder name extraction via .split(/[/\\]/).pop() — handles both POSIX and Windows.
createWorkspaceDashboard call wrapped in inner try/catch so welcome action doesn't fail if dashboard creation fails.
